parsed_review.summary:
CP00-755 is a descriptor-only RP24 Korean Legal Depth "P09 Closeout And Next Handoff" bridge pack (risk C, 80 units, RP24.P09.M06.S13-RP24.P09.M10.S10) that chains from CP00-754 and hands off to CP00-756 / RP25.P00.M00.S01. Independent read-only inspection of the in-scope source confirms the pack-level closeout claim is supported and no boundary is over-claimed.

Findings:
- Baseline vs implementation: KOREAN_LEGAL_CP755_PACK_BINDING and KOREAN_LEGAL_CP755_REQUIREMENTS match the prompt baseline exactly (risk C; unit_count 80; first/last unit; deliverable_distribution {claude_review:12, implementation:51, security_audit:8, test:4, ui:4, hermes_evidence:1}; micro_title_distribution {8,22,20,20,10}). Section-row constants resolve to 8+22+20+20+10 = 80 with correct titles per micro.
- No-write/no-real-data boundaries: KOREAN_LEGAL_NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION is spread into every artifact via freezeResult; createCaseSet/createDescriptor force descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, no raw/legal/credential/client payloads, no HWPX/court/litigation/corporate/clause runtime opening, no permission-decision detail, no audit event body, no cross-tenant access. validators.js validateDescriptor actively rejects drift on all of these.
- Authority boundary: descriptor authority_boundary sets claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-756. Claude packets are read_only with allowed tools Read/Grep/Glob and forbidden_review_evidence blockers (not_logged_in, malformed_json, usage_limit, tool_call_shaped_output, zero_byte_stdout, fenced_json_with_prose). Hermes packets emit_runtime_receipt=false (deterministic control-plane only; not product runtime).
- Tests: model.test.js asserts CP755 coverage (errors empty, by_deliverable matches), runtime-closed descriptor/packets (runtime_opening_pack_id CP00-756, next_subphase RP25.P00.M00.S01), and contract projection latest=CP755 with validation.valid=true.
- Contract: korean-legal-depth-contract.json latest_pack/current_pack/latest_projection are CP00-755 (KoreanLegalCp755P09CloseoutHandoffBridgeDescriptor), CP755 projection no_write_attestation all-false, validation.valid=true; generator script writes only the tracked contract artifact (build-time generation, not product state).
- Command evidence: all 25 commands exit_code 0, including all 10 required validators (closeout-pack-plan:validate, closeout-pack:validate, npm test, validate, spec:requirements:validate, weighted:validate, fullplan:validate, goal:closeout:validate, rp00:control-plane:validate) and git diff --check; no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false, gate_outcome pre_review_ladder_passed_waiting_for_claude_review.
- CP178+ hardening: readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, valid JSON result, 1248B stdout - not auth-failure/0-byte/malformed); prompt 2554B within 25600 limit; source_inspection_mode read_tools_used; summary_only prohibited and not used; review schema present; forbidden_tools_present=false; active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted empty.
- No over-claim: manifest (production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, status in_progress), construction-inspection (verdict PENDING, production_ready=false, pack_level_claude_review_completed=false), adjudication (in_progress), and claude-review-result (status pending, valid_review_receipts empty) are mutually consistent and correctly gate on exactly one valid hardened Claude receipt.

Claude remained a read-only independent reviewer and is not the final approver; the expected human/enterprise-trust authority boundary is outside this receipt and the pack makes no runtime or enterprise-trust claim. blocks_goal_closeout=false is set for this CP closeout goal only. No P0/P1/P2/P3 issues identified.
